nodes.name: 黑头白鹮
nodes.summary: 黑头白鹮（Black-headed ibis，学名 Threskiornis melanocephalus），分类上属于鹈形目，并归入鹮科，在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，已关联8个分布地点节点，常见栖息地包括湿地、沼泽。
nodes.shortSummary: 黑頭白䴉（學名：Threskiornis melanocephalus）為䴉科白䴉屬的鳥類，主要分布於北緯30度以南的南亞、東南亞靠近沿海地區，從印度西部向東延伸到日本。以及中國東北的鶴崗、佳木斯一帶。這個物種的頭部沒有羽毛而露出黑色的皮膚，其他部分以白色羽毛為主；這是其範圍內…

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The black-headed ibis (Threskiornis melanocephalus), also known as the Oriental white ibis, Indian white ibis, and black-necked ibis, is a species of wading bird of the ibis family Threskiornithidae which breeds in the South and Southeast Asia from India to the west and as far east as Japan. It is the only native ibis species in its range that has an overall white plumage with a black neck and head

links.label: 分布于

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: Though often referred to as a wetland species, the black-headed ibis forages in a range of natural and man-made habitats. This species of ibis nests only during the rainy season

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: In summer, they largely use and prefer natural marshes and fallow fields, but in the monsoon, spread out more evenly to also use a variety of agricultural fields. In landscapes that have more forests and rocky hills as in southern Rajasthan, wetlands are the preferred habitats year-round and there is little difference in habitats used in different seasons. In more urban landscapes, black-headed ibis abundance was positively associated with crop fields, wetlands and open areas.{{Cite journal |last1=Barik |first1=Souvik |last2=Saha |first2=Goutam Kumar |last3=Mazumdar |first3=Subhendu |date=2022 |title=How the Habitat Features Influence Black-Headed Ibis (Threskiornis melanocephalus) in a Suburban Area?
